veteran.reason: This fentanyl scheduling and research registration bill applies generally; veterans are not distinctly affected, and the mention of VA-conducted research pertains to institutions, not veterans as a group.

healthcare.reason: The bill overhauls Schedule I research registration procedures affecting clinical and pharmaceutical researchers, and involves practitioners conducting fentanyl-related research at HHS, DoD, and VA. Medical researchers are explicit subjects of the registration, inspection, and manufacturing-coincident-to-research provisions.

public_sector_first_responders.reason: Law enforcement and DEA/DOJ personnel administer the new scheduling and enforce expanded trafficking penalties, and government researchers at DoD/VA are named. The effect on enforcement and government scientific work is a first-order consequence.

target_groups.reason: The bill creates alternative registration, notification, and inspection procedures for practitioners and institutions conducting research with schedule I substances including fentanyl-related substances.
